intent: a personal dashboard for tracking long-term health experiments

body.intent: a personal dashboard for tracking long-term health experiments

draft.title: a personal dashboard for tracking long-term health experiments
draft.summary: Drafting your prototype from the swipes…
draft.html:
a personal dashboard for tracking long-term health experiments

Building your first draft…

data.intent: a personal dashboard for tracking long-term health experiments

facade.hypothesis: Users prefer chronological narrative over static snapshots for tracking experiments over weeks/months
facade.axisTargeted: temporal structure — linear timeline vs circular/aggregate dashboard
facade.label: Timeline view
facade.acceptImplies: Lean into story-driven progress: scrollable experiment timelines, milestone markers, before/after narratives
facade.rejectImplies: Lean into state-driven overview: current metrics grid, summary cards, status at a glance

facade.hypothesis: Users tracking health experiments prefer high contrast and clear readability over softer, lower-contrast designs
facade.label: Dark cards, light text
facade.acceptImplies: Explore dark mode, bold typography, and high-contrast data visualizations for the dashboard
facade.rejectImplies: Explore softer palettes, lower contrast, and gentler visual hierarchies—perhaps muted colors with lighter backgrounds

evidence.hypothesis: Users prefer chronological narrative over static snapshots for tracking experiments over weeks/months
evidence.implication: Lean into story-driven progress: scrollable experiment timelines, milestone markers, before/after narratives

facade.hypothesis: A health dashboard with diffused, gentle lighting and subtle depth creates a calming, intimate atmosphere that encourages daily reflection rather than clinical tracking
facade.axisTargeted: warm vs cool + intimate vs expansive
facade.label: Soft shadows
facade.acceptImplies: User gravitates toward gentle, forgiving visual language — likely values introspection and ease over precision theatre or data minimalism
facade.rejectImplies: User prefers either bolder contrast (cool precision) or flatter, more direct layouts — suggests emphasis on clarity and action over ambient mood

facade.hypothesis: A persistent left sidebar for experiment navigation paired with card-based layouts for viewing individual experiment data will feel organized and scannable for health tracking.
facade.axisTargeted: Navigation structure (sidebar vs tabs) + content density (cards vs lists)
facade.label: Sidebar with cards
facade.acceptImplies: User values persistent context and visual modularity; likely prefers a dashboard where experiments stay accessible without navigation friction.
facade.rejectImplies: User may prefer a flatter, more linear structure; tabs or a full-width card grid might resonate better than embedded sidebar patterns.

axes.label: Whether user prefers minimal clinical aesthetics vs. warm, human-centered visual design for health data
Whether user wants data-dense dashboard layout vs. progressive disclosure with one metric at a time
axes.poleA: All at once or step by step?

axes.label: Whether user responds to motivational/encouraging tone vs. neutral, factual reporting voice
Whether user wants celebratory, gamified feedback vs. calm, meditative tracking environment
Whether user prefers raw numbers and charts vs. narrative summaries and insights
Whether user wants instant feedback on actions vs. slow, contemplative animations and transitions

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers minimal clinical aesthetics vs. warm, human-centered visual design for health data
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Iris

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants data-dense dashboard layout vs. progressive disclosure with one metric at a time
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Prism

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user responds to motivational/encouraging tone vs. neutral, factual reporting voice
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Lumen

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants celebratory, gamified feedback vs. calm, meditative tracking environment
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Aura

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers raw numbers and charts vs. narrative summaries and insights
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Facet

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants instant feedback on actions vs. slow, contemplative animations and transitions
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Echo

facade.hypothesis: Users prefer fluid, continuous scrolling transitions over instant jumps when reviewing multi-week experiment data, reducing cognitive load and creating sense of continuity.
facade.axisTargeted: Motion continuity — eased scrolling vs snap-to-section
facade.label: Smooth page scroll
facade.acceptImplies: Prioritize fluid animations, momentum scrolling, and ease-out transitions for exploring historical data; users value the feeling of moving through time.
facade.rejectImplies: Users want snappy, instant navigation between experiments or data points; they prefer click-based jumps and hate animation overhead on small screens.

facade.hypothesis: Users prefer a friendly, conversational voice that feels like a supportive friend rather than a clinical instrument when logging health data.
facade.axisTargeted: Voice and personality — friendly vs professional
facade.label: Casual check-in tone
facade.acceptImplies: Design copy and microcopy with warmth, encouragement, and informal language. Use second-person, occasional humor, and celebratory language around milestones.
facade.rejectImplies: Shift toward professional, neutral, informative tone. Remove personality markers. Focus on clarity and precision over relatability.

facade.hypothesis: Users tracking health experiments prefer scannable key metrics and summary stats over dense data tables, enabling quick status checks without requiring deep analysis
facade.axisTargeted: Information density and cognitive load — sparse metrics vs. comprehensive data display
facade.label: Numbers at a glance
facade.acceptImplies: Emphasize metric cards, big numbers, progress percentages, and KPI summaries as primary UI elements; minimize tables and detailed breakdowns in default view
facade.rejectImplies: Users need access to granular data and full context upfront; design should expose complete datasets, detailed tables, and comparison views as the main interaction layer

draft.summary: A personal health dashboard for monitoring long-term experiments with visual progress tracking, experiment cards, and key metrics at a glance.
